title: "Bitcoin ATMs tied to millions in scams now banned in an Eastern WA city - Tri-City Herald"

---

The city of Kennewick in Eastern Washington has enacted a ban on Bitcoin ATMs following their association with millions of dollars in scams. This decision comes amid growing concerns over the role of cryptocurrency in facilitating fraudulent activities, particularly in areas with high ATM concentrations. Local authorities have cited the need to protect residents from financial exploitation as a driving force behind the ban.

The implications for the cryptocurrency market are significant, as regulatory scrutiny increases in various jurisdictions. The move could signal a broader trend of local governments tightening regulations on cryptocurrency transactions, particularly in areas where scams are prevalent. This could impact the operational landscape for Bitcoin ATMs and potentially lead to decreased consumer confidence in crypto assets.

Market professionals should monitor similar regulatory developments, as they could influence Bitcoin's adoption rates and overall market sentiment. The Kennewick ban highlights the need for robust consumer protections in the rapidly evolving cryptocurrency space.

Source: [news.google.com](https://news.google.?oc=5)
